chapter_summary: This chapter tells us why God gave us the book of Proverbs. Learning wisdom helps us live right and please God. The best place to start is by respecting God — that is where real knowledge begins. The chapter also warns us not to follow friends who want us to do wrong. At the end, Wisdom calls out to everyone, asking them to listen and choose the right path. Those who listen will be safe. Those who ignore her will face trouble.
main_lesson: Respect God and listen to wisdom. Say no to people who want you to do wrong.
memory_verse: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge.
parent_guide: Chapter 1 introduces the purpose of Proverbs: to grow wise, just, and understanding people. Three key themes: (1) The 'fear of the Lord' means deep respect and awe for God — it is the starting point of all true wisdom. (2) Peer pressure is real even for young children — Solomon's warning about sinners who 'entice' is timely. (3) Wisdom is personified as a woman calling loudly — help children understand that God's wisdom is always available; the question is whether we listen. Encourage your child to ask a wise adult before making hard decisions.
application_for_children: This week, practice saying 'no thank you' when someone asks you to do something wrong. Tell a parent what happened. Pray each morning: 'God, help me choose wisdom today.'

verses.kjv: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;
verses.little_bible: These are wise sayings from Solomon. He was a great king.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Solomon wrote wise things for us.
verses.meaning: God gave Solomon great wisdom. He wrote these sayings so we could learn to live well.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for wise teachers.
verses.discussion_question: Who teaches you good things?
verses.family_discussion: Who is someone wise that you love?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a parent or teacher one question about God today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A wise king sitting on a throne, smiling, holding an open scroll

verses.kjv: To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;
verses.little_bible: These sayings help us learn wisdom. They show us what is right.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: These sayings help us learn good things.
verses.meaning: God wants us to grow in wisdom — to understand good things and learn how to live the right way.
verses.prayer: God, help me love wisdom.
verses.discussion_question: What does being wise mean?
verses.family_discussion: Can you name one wise thing you learned this week?
verses.do_it_today: Read one proverb together and talk about what it means.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open Bible with sunlight shining on the pages, a child reading

verses.kjv: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;
verses.little_bible: These sayings teach us to do what is right. They teach us to be fair to everyone.
verses.meaning: God's wisdom teaches us to act justly — to be fair, honest, and kind to everyone.
verses.prayer: God, help me be fair and right.
verses.discussion_question: What does being fair mean?

verses.kjv: To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.
verses.little_bible: These sayings help even young children. They help us make good choices.
verses.meaning: Wisdom is for everyone, even young people. It helps us think before we act so we do not make foolish mistakes.
verses.prayer: God, help me think before I choose.
verses.discussion_question: How do you make good choices?

verses.kjv: A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:
verses.little_bible: Wise people keep on listening. They never stop learning.
verses.meaning: Being wise does not mean you know everything. Wise people never stop listening and growing.
verses.prayer: God, help me keep on learning.
verses.discussion_question: Why should we never stop learning?

verses.kjv: To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.
verses.little_bible: These sayings help us understand hard things. God helps us understand.
verses.meaning: Some of God's wisdom is hard to understand right away. But if we keep learning, He helps us understand deeper things.
verses.prayer: God, help me when I don't understand.
verses.discussion_question: Who helps you when things are hard?

verses.kjv: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
verses.little_bible: Respecting God is where wisdom begins. People who ignore God are foolish.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Love and respect God. That is where wisdom starts.
verses.meaning: True wisdom and knowledge start with respecting and honoring God. People who do not want to listen to God miss out on real wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, help me always respect You.
verses.discussion_question: How do we show respect to God?
verses.family_discussion: What is one way we can honor God together today?
verses.do_it_today: Say a prayer together right now to honor God.
verses.action_challenge: Can everyone say together: 'God, we respect You!'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child kneeling in prayer with warm golden light coming from above

verses.kjv: My son, hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
verses.little_bible: Listen to what your father teaches you. Do not forget what your mother says.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Listen to Mommy and Daddy. They love you.
verses.meaning: God wants children to listen to and obey their parents. Parents teach us important things about life and about God.
verses.prayer: God, help me obey my parents.
verses.discussion_question: Why does God want us to obey parents?
verses.family_discussion: What is one way to obey right now?
verses.do_it_today: When Mommy or Daddy calls you today, answer quickly and cheerfully.
verses.action_challenge: Parents: share one thing you learned from your own parents.
verses.illustration_prompt: A parent and child sitting together on a couch, the child listening with a smile

verses.kjv: For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.
verses.little_bible: When you obey your parents, it is beautiful. It shows who you are on the inside.
verses.meaning: Listening to and obeying your parents makes you beautiful on the inside. Good character is like something wonderful you wear.
verses.prayer: God, make me good on the inside.
verses.discussion_question: What makes a person beautiful on the inside?

verses.kjv: My son, if sinners entice thee, consent thou not.
verses.little_bible: If someone asks you to do wrong, say no.
verses.meaning: Sometimes people will try to talk us into doing bad things. God wants us to be strong and say no.
verses.prayer: God, help me say no to wrong.
verses.discussion_question: How do you say no to wrong?

verses.kjv: If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause:
verses.little_bible: Some people want to hurt those who did nothing wrong. Do not join them.
verses.meaning: Wicked people try to get others to join them in doing cruel and harmful things, even to innocent people.
verses.prayer: God, keep me from hurting others.
verses.discussion_question: What if someone asks you to be mean?

verses.kjv: Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:
verses.little_bible: They want to destroy others completely. Their hearts are cruel.
verses.meaning: Wicked people do not care about the harm they cause. Their hearts are full of cruelty.
verses.prayer: God, give me a kind heart.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it wrong to want to hurt others?

verses.kjv: We shall find all precious merchandise, we shall fill our houses with spoil:
verses.little_bible: They say, 'We will take what we want.' Taking from others is wrong.
verses.meaning: Wicked people think it is fine to take things from others. But getting things by doing wrong is never truly good.
verses.prayer: God, help me be glad with what I have.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it wrong to take things?

verses.kjv: Cast in thy lot among us; let us all have one purse:
verses.little_bible: They say, 'Join us and do wrong together.' That is dangerous.
verses.meaning: Wicked people make doing wrong seem fun and rewarding. They try to get you to join their plans.
verses.prayer: God, help me choose good friends.
verses.discussion_question: How do you know if friends are good?

verses.kjv: My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:
verses.little_bible: Do not go with them. Stay on the right path.
verses.meaning: God's instruction is clear — do not follow people who are doing evil. Stay far away from their ways.
verses.prayer: God, help me walk on Your path.
verses.discussion_question: What is the brave thing when friends go wrong?

verses.kjv: For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.
verses.little_bible: These people do not walk toward trouble. They run.
verses.meaning: People who choose evil rush into it eagerly. Doing wrong gets faster and worse when we do not stop.
verses.prayer: God, help me run toward what is good.
verses.discussion_question: Why is rushing into bad things dangerous?

verses.kjv: Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird.
verses.little_bible: Even birds avoid traps they can see. Listen to warnings.
verses.meaning: Even a bird is smart enough to avoid a trap it can see. When we are warned about danger, we should pay attention.
verses.prayer: God, help me listen to warnings.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do when warned of danger?

verses.kjv: And they lay wait for their own blood; they lurk privily for their own lives.
verses.little_bible: But evil people actually trap themselves. Their bad choices hurt them.
verses.meaning: When we choose to do wrong, we are really hurting ourselves most of all. Sin always has a cost.
verses.prayer: God, help me see that wrong choices hurt me.
verses.discussion_question: Can you see how a bad choice hurt someone?

verses.kjv: So are the ways of every one that is greedy of gain; such covetousness taketh away the life of the owners thereof.
verses.little_bible: Everyone who is greedy ends up hurt. Greed destroys people.
verses.meaning: Wanting more and more in a greedy way is dangerous. It leads people away from God and from what truly matters.
verses.prayer: God, help me not be greedy.
verses.discussion_question: Why is always wanting more a bad thing?

verses.kjv: Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:
verses.little_bible: Wisdom is like a person calling out loud. She wants everyone to hear.
verses.meaning: God's wisdom is not hidden — it is calling out to all of us, every day. We only need to choose to listen.
verses.prayer: God, help me hear Your wisdom today.
verses.discussion_question: Where can we learn wisdom?

verses.kjv: She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, saying,
verses.little_bible: Wisdom calls out where many people are. She wants all people to hear.
verses.meaning: Wisdom is available to all people everywhere. God makes sure His wisdom reaches everyone who is willing to listen.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that wisdom is for me.
verses.discussion_question: Can anyone learn wisdom, not just adults?

verses.kjv: How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and the scorners delight in their scorning, and fools hate knowledge?
verses.little_bible: Wisdom asks: will you stay foolish? When will you choose to learn?
verses.meaning: God wants us to grow up and choose wisdom. Staying foolish on purpose, or making fun of wisdom, makes God sad.
verses.prayer: God, I do not want to be foolish.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to refuse to learn?

verses.kjv: Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom says: turn around and listen. God will fill you with understanding.
verses.meaning: When we turn away from foolishness and listen to God's wisdom, He fills us with His Spirit and understanding.
verses.prayer: God, I want to turn toward You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to do right?

verses.kjv: Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded;
verses.little_bible: Wisdom says: I have been calling to you. But you would not listen.
verses.meaning: God calls out to us and reaches out to help us. It makes Him sad when we choose to ignore Him.
verses.prayer: God, help me always listen to You.
verses.discussion_question: How do you feel when no one listens to you?

verses.kjv: But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:
verses.little_bible: They threw away God's good advice. They did not want to be taught.
verses.meaning: Ignoring God's teaching and correction is very serious. When we refuse correction, we are walking away from wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, help me welcome Your correction.
verses.discussion_question: Is it easy or hard to be corrected?

verses.kjv: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh;
verses.little_bible: When trouble comes, wisdom says: I could not help you. You would not listen.
verses.meaning: When people refuse wisdom and trouble comes because of their foolish choices, wisdom cannot protect them the way it could have. This is a serious warning — it shows how important it is to choose wisdom early.
verses.prayer: God, help me choose wisdom now.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it better to ask for help early?

verses.kjv: When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you.
verses.little_bible: Trouble can come fast, like a big storm. It can hurt very much.
verses.meaning: The trouble that comes from ignoring wisdom can feel like a terrible storm — sudden, frightening, and overwhelming.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust You in hard times.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do to get ready for hard times?

verses.kjv: Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:
verses.little_bible: Then they will ask for wisdom. But it may be too late.
verses.meaning: There is a cost to saying no to God's wisdom for too long. Waiting until trouble comes makes it much harder to find peace and direction.
verses.prayer: God, help me seek You right now.
verses.discussion_question: Why should we not wait to seek God?

verses.kjv: For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the LORD:
verses.little_bible: This happened because they would not learn. They chose not to respect God.
verses.meaning: Trouble comes when we turn away from God's knowledge and choose to ignore Him. Every bad outcome has a root — and often that root is turning away from God.
verses.prayer: God, I choose to respect You today.
verses.discussion_question: Do we choose to respect God every day?

verses.kjv: They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof.
verses.little_bible: They did not want any advice. They hated being told they were wrong.
verses.meaning: Refusing guidance and hating correction is a sign of foolishness. People who cannot accept being wrong cut themselves off from wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, help me be easy to teach.
verses.discussion_question: Does it take courage to admit being wrong?

verses.kjv: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own devices.
verses.little_bible: So they got what they chose. Choices always come back to us.
verses.meaning: Our choices have consequences. When we make foolish choices, we experience the results of those choices.
verses.prayer: God, help me make good choices now.
verses.discussion_question: Can you name a good or bad choice result?

verses.kjv: For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.
verses.little_bible: Foolish people walk away from wisdom. They end up getting hurt.
verses.meaning: Turning away from God's wisdom leads to destruction. Even temporary success from wrong choices will not last.
verses.prayer: God, help me stay close to wisdom.
verses.discussion_question: Why does foolishness always lead to trouble?

verses.kjv: But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil.
verses.little_bible: Those who listen to wisdom will be safe. They will not be afraid.
verses.meaning: The wonderful promise of wisdom is safety and peace. When we follow God's wisdom, He gives us a life free from fear.
verses.prayer: God, I want to listen to Your wisdom.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to be safe?
